classifier.reasoning: This is a universal NPU migration pitfall: torchaudio, torchvision, and other PyTorch ecosystem packages on PyPI default to CUDA-linked wheels. The fix pattern (detect import failure, reinstall with +cpu suffix) applies to every NPU migration that uses these packages. The solution runs on NPU directly (no CPU fallback) and is mechanically repeatable across projects. The fix is procedural: after pip install, run an import test; if libtorch_cuda.so error surfaces, reinstall from the CPU wheel index with the +cpu suffix. This is a deterministic detection→repair rule suitable for automated enforcement in the dependency-install phase.

title: torchaudio defaults to CUDA-linked wheel — reinstall from PyTorch CPU index
problem_description: pip install torchaudio from PyPI resolves to a CUDA-linked wheel that imports libtorch_cuda.so at runtime, causing ImportError/OSError on NPU-only systems. This affects all PyTorch ecosystem packages (torchaudio, torchvision) and is not caught during pip install because the CUDA build metadata is embedded in the wheel name but not validated at install time.
rough_fix_approach: After pip install torchaudio in Phase 2, detect the CUDA linking by running `python -c 'import torchaudio'` in the venv. If import fails with a libtorch_cuda.so error, reinstall from the PyTorch CPU wheel index: `pip install torchaudio==<version>+cpu --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu`. The `+cpu` suffix forces selection of the CPU-build wheel which has no CUDA dependency.
